target.title: fix(reply): retry stalled compacted direct sessions after reset
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #90516.

Long-lived direct chat sessions with compaction history can wedge during an embedded model call. OpenClaw already detects the blocked/aborted run, but before this change it surfaced the generic `LLM request failed.` payload to the user instead of rotating the hot session and retrying the same turn once.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a narrow automatic recovery path in the reply runner for compacted direct sessions when the first run is aborted, blocked, has no fallback path, no pending tool work, and no visible/side-effect delivery evidence. When those guards match, OpenClaw resets only the hot session entry, retries the same inbound turn once, records lifecycle events, and suppresses duplicate persistence of the replayed user prompt in the fresh transcript.

The block reply pipeline now exposes pending delivery state so the reset decision can flush any in-flight block delivery before deciding that the first attempt produced no user-visible output.

## User Impact

A Telegram or other direct-chat user whose compacted session wedges should get the recovered assistant reply after one automatic reset/retry instead of immediately seeing `LLM request failed.`. The guard avoids retrying if the first attempt already delivered visible output, used fallback, sent via messaging tools, has pending tools, or is not a compacted direct session.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts` - passed, 50 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` - passed, 214 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/block-reply-pipeline.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/block-reply-pipeline.multi-message.test.ts` - passed, 28 tests.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/block-reply-pipeline.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts` - passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/block-reply-pipeline.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts` - passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` - passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-90516.tsbuildinfo` - passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-90516.tsbuildinfo` - passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

Known proof gap: `agent-runner-payloads.test.ts` hit a local timeout in the unrelated existing `delivers distinct same-target replies when message tool target provider is generic` case; the only changes in that file are type-shape updates adding `hasPendingDelivery` to block-pipeline mocks, and core test tsgo plus focused oxlint cover that interface surface.
